questions.question: What percentage of late invoice payments are resolved with a single follow-up reminder?
questions.explanation: 82% of late invoice payments resolve with a single follow-up reminder. Most overdue invoices aren't clients avoiding payment — they're people who got busy or forgot. Automation catches them before it becomes a relationship problem.

questions.question: For SaaS businesses, what percentage of all churn comes from involuntary churn (failed payments)?
questions.explanation: Involuntary churn (failed payments) accounts for 20-40% of all SaaS churn. Most of those customers didn't mean to cancel — their card expired or a payment failed. Dunning automation is the single highest-ROI system in this course.

questions.question: How much revenue can Stripe Smart Retries alone recover from initially failed payments?
questions.explanation: Stripe Smart Retries alone recover 10-25% of initially failed payments by retrying at the optimal time. Adding Baremetrics Recover on top recovers an additional 5-15%. For a $10K MRR SaaS, this is $1,500-4,000/month saved.

questions.question: The SaaS failed payment problem and the service business overdue invoice problem require the same solution.
questions.explanation: False. They are fundamentally different problems. SaaS failed payments are system problems (expired cards, bank declines) solved with automatic retries and card update prompts. Service overdue invoices are human behavior problems solved with escalating reminder sequences and personal follow-up.

questions.question: Do you currently have a dunning or collections system in place? Estimate how much revenue you might be losing to failed payments or overdue invoices. What specific automation would you implement first?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response honestly assesses their current dunning setup (or lack thereof), estimates potential revenue loss using their actual numbers (MRR, churn rate, overdue invoices), and proposes a specific first automation step (Stripe Smart Retries, reminder sequence, or pre-expiry notifications).
